s01_birth.subject: Isolated slave cabin under a starless sky
s01_birth.action: Rain streaks across a low Virginia cabin; a single lantern glows in one window. Sky is ink-black, no stars.
s01_birth.dialogue_excerpt: They say he came on the blackest night the county ever saw — a night so dark the stars themselves stepped back to watch.
s01_birth.composition_notes: Cabin small in lower third, vast black sky dominating frame. Lantern is the only warm point of light. Mythic scale.

s01_birth.subject: Cabin interior — midwife and laboring mother
s01_birth.action: Midwife bends over the straw mattress, sleeves rolled. Mother grips the bedpost, sweat-slick, jaw clenched against a contraction.
s01_birth.composition_notes: Single lantern key from frame-left carves deep shadows. Tin-roof rain texture audible. Earthy, Caravaggio palette.

s01_birth.subject: John Henry's mother
s01_birth.action: Her knuckles whiten on the bedpost; she throws her head back, breath ragged. A newborn cry tears the silence.
s01_birth.composition_notes: Hard rim light along brow and jaw, eyes lost in shadow. Sweat catches the lantern.

s01_birth.camera_move: slow tilt up as midwife lifts the child
s01_birth.subject: Midwife raising the newborn toward the rafters
s01_birth.action: The midwife lifts the impossibly large, broad-chested infant. Her face goes slack with awe.
s01_birth.dialogue_excerpt: Lord. This child come into the world full-grown.
s01_birth.composition_notes: Infant silhouetted against lantern halo — biblical framing. Midwife's lips clearly visible for lip-sync.

s01_birth.subject: The newborn's eyes
s01_birth.action: The baby's eyes are open — dark, ancient, knowing. They do not blink.
s01_birth.composition_notes: Razor-thin focus on the irises. Lantern reflected as twin pinpricks. Mythic stillness.

s01_birth.angle: low, looking up past the fist toward the rafters
s01_birth.camera_move: slow push-in on the tiny hand
s01_birth.subject: Newborn's fist rising
s01_birth.action: The infant's tiny fist lifts toward the dark rafters, fingers closing on empty air as if gripping an unseen hammer handle.
s01_birth.dialogue_excerpt: A hammer... gonna be the death of me.
s01_birth.composition_notes: Fist centered against black rafters. Voice is a low, impossible whisper — infant on screen, but mouth not emphasized; line reads as prophecy over the gesture. Thunder rolls beneath.

s02_growing_up.subject: Twelve-year-old John Henry in a sun-baked cotton field
s02_growing_up.action: Boy hoists a heavy oak cider barrel onto one shoulder, grinning wide at off-screen field hands; cotton bolls glow white around him.
s02_growing_up.dialogue_excerpt: He grew like an unshaded hickory — straight, hard, reaching for sky no one had promised him.
s02_growing_up.composition_notes: Boy framed against blazing sky, barrel silhouetted high; heat haze ripples the horizon. Golden-hour key, dust motes in the air.

s02_growing_up.subject: Slack-jawed older field hands
s02_growing_up.action: Two weathered men in straw hats stare up, mouths slightly open, sweat on their brows; one shakes his head in disbelief.
s02_growing_up.composition_notes: Tight two-shot reaction. Shallow focus, cotton field bokeh behind. Beat of awe.

s02_growing_up.subject: Sixteen-year-old John Henry at a sawmill
s02_growing_up.action: Taller now, shirt clinging with sweat, he hauls an axe up over his head and brings it down — a thick timber splits clean in a burst of pale chips.
s02_growing_up.composition_notes: Sawmill interior, shafts of dusty light through plank walls. Stacks of cut lumber frame him. Strong rim light from a high window.

s02_growing_up.subject: John Henry, sixteen, axe resting on shoulder
s02_growing_up.action: He glances aside with an easy half-smile, wipes his brow with the back of a forearm, speaks without strain.
s02_growing_up.dialogue_excerpt: Step aside, uncle. Let a working man work.
s02_growing_up.composition_notes: Sawdust drifts past lens. Warm tungsten-like daylight on his face; background falls into shadow. Confidence, not arrogance.

s02_growing_up.subject: Crowd of freedmen on a dirt road; John Henry at a crossroads
s02_growing_up.action: Freedmen with bundles and children scatter down the road in every direction; John Henry stands still at the fork, satchel at his feet, head tilted as if listening.
s02_growing_up.dialogue_excerpt: Something inside me said stop, boy. Rest. But a dollar and a quarter a day don't whisper — it hollers.
s02_growing_up.composition_notes: Wide vista — dust kicked up by departing crowd, distant pines. John Henry centered, the still point. Late-afternoon light, long shadows pointing toward the smoke on the horizon.

s02_growing_up.subject: John Henry from behind, facing distant railroad smoke
s02_growing_up.action: He bends, shoulders the battered satchel, and starts walking toward black smoke columns rising on the horizon — railroad camp scaffolding faint in the haze.
s02_growing_up.dialogue_excerpt: Hammer's waitin' on me. I ain't gonna keep it waitin' long.
s02_growing_up.composition_notes: Backlit silhouette against amber sky and smoke. We hear his quiet line as he moves — face turned in three-quarter so lips are visible at the start. Foreshadowing weight in his stride.

s03_brush_arbor.subject: Brush-arbor church interior
s03_brush_arbor.action: Sunlight knifes through pine-board slats. Dust motes drift. Congregation sways in pews, hands lifted.
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: God-rays cutting diagonal across frame. Silhouetted heads in foreground, lit slats above. Establishes heat and reverence.

s03_brush_arbor.subject: Polly Ann singing
s03_brush_arbor.action: Polly Ann in a yellow kerchief, small and straight-backed, lifts a hymn line. Her throat works; her eyes are closed.
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: Backlight halos the yellow kerchief. Shallow focus; pews soft behind. Warm key from a slat of sun across her cheek.

s03_brush_arbor.subject: John Henry standing in the back row
s03_brush_arbor.action: From the back row John Henry rises — enormous, broad as a doorframe. His bass answers her line, low as river stone.
s03_brush_arbor.dialogue_excerpt: (deep bass hymn response, rumbling under hers)
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: He fills the frame top to bottom. Slat-light bars his chest and face. Smaller worshippers in foreground emphasize his scale.

s03_brush_arbor.camera_move: rack focus from Polly Ann to John Henry across the pews
s03_brush_arbor.subject: Polly Ann's eyes finding John Henry's
s03_brush_arbor.action: Polly Ann's eyes open mid-hymn and find his across the pews. She does not look away. Focus pulls to him; he holds her gaze.
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: Heads of congregation soft-blurred between them. Their voices braid on the soundtrack. The look does the work.

s03_brush_arbor.subject: John Henry and Polly Ann on the red dirt road
s03_brush_arbor.action: They walk side by side down a red dirt road. Pine shadows long across the clay. Neither speaks. Her hand brushes his once.
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: Golden hour. His silhouette dwarfs hers. Long pine shadows striping the road. Cicadas, no dialogue. The brush of hands is the whole shot.

s03_brush_arbor.subject: Polly Ann at the weathered gate
s03_brush_arbor.action: At the gate Polly Ann stops, turns, studies him a long moment — like measuring timber.
s03_brush_arbor.dialogue_excerpt: You sing like a man who means to stay.
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: His shoulder dark in foreground left. She is small, framed by gate posts. Warm low sun on her face. Beat of silence before the line.

s03_brush_arbor.subject: John Henry answering
s03_brush_arbor.action: John Henry looks down at her, hat in his hands. He answers quietly.
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: Tight on his face. Sweat at the temple. Eyes steady. The promise lands soft.

s03_brush_arbor.camera_move: static, holds as she exits frame
s03_brush_arbor.subject: Polly Ann at the gate, decision made
s03_brush_arbor.action: She nods once — the decision already made — and slips through the gate without looking back. The gate sways shut.
s03_brush_arbor.composition_notes: Hold on the empty gate after she passes through. Pine shadows. John Henry's hand visible at frame edge, hat held. A held breath.
